Subject: Property of Various Owners, featuring the Estate of Lt. Col. Arthur
Message:
Dear Collector,

Litchfield County Auctions of Connecticut, a Sothebys.com Associate, is pleased to offer property from various private collections and estates, including the Estate of Lt. Col. Arthur B. Scherrer of Albany, New York.   Nestled in an impressive row of town houses facing Washington Park, Albany, New York, is 439 State Street, a brick and rusticated brown stone house built for Albert V. Benson of the China Tea Co. around 1880.  Quite possibly the work of architect Albert Fuller, the building houses the presently-offered collection of the late Col. Arthur Scherrer.   There will be an exhibition of highlights from this sale at 439 State Street, Albany, New York, Friday, November 30 through Sunday, December 2.  Please click the link below to go directly to the sale.

https://news.sothebys.com/cgi-bin2/flo?y=eFEM0DBy5t0OX0BDhw0Ap


Highlights in this auction include a strong grouping of 19th century marble-top and ormolu-mounted furniture in the 18th century style.  A wonderful S. Kirk & Son sterling-silver repousse tea and coffee service,  cut-glass and ormolu chandeliers and sconces from the early 20th century are on offer as well.  Meissen, KPM, and English porcelain dinner services, clocks, bronzes, porcelain and portrait miniatures from the 19th century are also included in this eclectic auction. Visit the sale now; bidding ends December 6.

https://news.sothebys.com/cgi-bin2/flo?y=eFEM0DBy5t0OX0BDhw0Ap
